response: Jem Finer played the banjo in the band The Pogues. He was a banjo player and composed songs for the band, including "Once Upon a Time," "Fairytale of New York," and "Misty Morning, Albert Bridge." He also contributed to the band's later works, such as "Sunny Side of the Street" and "Edith Walks," and was one of the founding members of The Pogues.
